mechanism: Contains asarone-related phenylpropanoids and other volatile constituents, but no high-confidence human mechanism is established. Reported CNS and digestive effects are based mainly on traditional use and preclinical pharmacology.

contraindications: Use caution with prescription medications and chronic conditions; monitor for intolerance.
discontinue if hypersensitivity or allergic symptoms occur
safetyNotes: Safety data are limited; possible adverse effects include GI upset and dizziness with concentrated preparations. Interaction categories: CNS-active sedatives/intoxicants (possible additive sedation) and medications with narrow safety margins due to uncertain pharmacokinetics. Avoid in pregnancy/lactation pending better safety data.
description: Acorus americanus (American sweet flag) is a North American aromatic wetland herb used traditionally for digestive and calming purposes; modern human clinical evidence is limited.
